articles.title: WTI Price Forecast: Struggles to reclaim $100, outlook remains firm
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), futures on NYMEX, corrects to near $97.20 during the European trading session on Wednesday. The Oil price gives back some of its recent gains as escalating hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) bets have raised concerns over the oil demand outlook.

articles.title: US Dollar Index: Hawkish repricing supports DXY – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC’s Christopher Wong notes the US Dollar (USD) strengthened after hotter United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI) data lifted UST yields and revived Fed hike expectations, with focus now on US Producer Price Index (PPI).

articles.title: Gold struggles to lure buyers amid bullish USD, Fed rate hike bets, ahead of US PPI
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) sticks to modest intraday losses through the early European session on Wednesday, albeit it lacks bearish conviction and remains confined within the previous day's broader trading range.

articles.title: Euro softens against British Pound ahead of Eurozone GDP data
articles.expand: The EUR/GBP cross loses momentum to near 0.8660 during the early European trading hours on Wednesday. The Euro (EUR) weakens against the British Pound (GBP) due to fears over the Eurozone's economic exposure to energy shocks from the Middle East and stalled US-Iran peace negotiations.

articles.title: Indian Rupee shows little strength as import duty on Gold and Silver hikes to 15%
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) exhibits mild strength against the US Dollar (USD) in the opening session on Wednesday. The USD/INR pair trades mildly lower to near 95.60 as the Indian Rupee gains, following the increase in import duty on Gold and Silver to 15% from 6% by the Indian government.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar holds near lows as high US yields buoy the Greenback
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) is practically flat against the Canadian Dollar (CAD) on Wednesday, hovering at a short distance from the four-week high, at 1.3724.

articles.expand: CPI +2.2% vs +2.2% y/y prelim Prior +1.7% HICP +2.5% vs +2.5% y/y prelim Prior +2.0% The monthly change itself also shows a 1% increase in headline inflation, leading to the annual estimate to cross back above the 2% mark in April. Of note, energy prices once again picked up in recording a 4.7% increase on the month after jumping up by 8.9% in Marc...

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the French final CPI report and the second estimate of Eurozone Q1 GDP. The data isn't going to change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will likely be muted. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the main highligh...

articles.title: Japanese Yen extends decline against USD amid rising hawkish Fed bets
articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) trades lower against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Wednesday, with the USD/JPY pair edging higher to near 157.70.

articles.title: US Dollar: Stronger on hot inflation and yields – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Michael Wan describes how a stronger US Dollar environment emerged as United States (US) April Consumer Price Index (CPI) surprised to the upside and US yields climbed.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Holds modest upside while staying anchored above 100-day EMA support
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair trades on a positive note around 1.3550 during the early European trading hours on Wednesday.

articles.title: Markets Hold Breath Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit as Hormuz Crisis Nears Critical Point
articles.expand: Markets are staying in a cautious holding pattern as investors awaited the highly anticipated Trump-Xi summit later this week, increasingly viewing the Beijing meeting as the decisive geopolitical checkpoint for both the Strait of Hormuz crisis and broader global trade relations. While financial markets remained relatively calm overall, underlying ...

articles.title: ECB's Villeroy: Central bank must be ready to intervene on second-round effects
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member and Governor of the Bank of France, François Villeroy de Galhau, said on Wednesday that the central bank must be ready to intervene in the second round effects.

articles.title: ECB's Villeroy: We don't have enough information on core inflation yet
articles.expand: The ECB must be ready to intervene on second-round effects We don't have enough information on core inflation yet ECB's Villeroy reiterates his cautious stance on interest rates as he's waiting for more data to confirm the need for a rate hike. Earlier this month, Villeroy highlighted that the ECB currently lacks a "critical mass of data" to justif...

articles.title: Fed still sidelined even as US inflation picks up in April - CIBC
articles.expand: In case you missed it: US April CPI 3.8% y/y vs 3.7% expected It was no surprise that headline inflation got another bump up on the back of higher energy prices. However, core prices were also seen moving up as shelter prices in particular reaccelerated on the month. But even with that aside, there were higher price pressures in broader categories ...

articles.title: WTI corrects to near $97.20 amid oil demand concerns, Trump-Xi meeting in focus
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), futures on NYMEX, is down 1.5% to near $97.20 during the Asian trading session on Wednesday.

articles.title: New Zealand Inflation Expectations Jump as RBNZ OCR Outlook Turns Hawkish
articles.expand: New Zealand’s latest RBNZ Survey of Expectations showed a sharp rise in near-term inflation expectations, reinforcing concerns that price pressures may remain elevated even as growth slows. One-year-ahead CPI inflation expectations jumped from 2.59% to 3.41%, while two-year expectations rose from 2.37% to 2.53%, moving further above the Reserve Ban...

articles.expand: The OECD secretary-general said the BOJ is not clearly behind the curve on rates and called for reform of international trading rules, describing the upcoming Trump-Xi summit as an important part of that process. Summary: The BOJ is not considered clearly behind the curve on monetary policy, with inflation expectations anchored and wage dynamics co...

articles.title: OECD Sees BoJ Raising Rates to 2% by End-2027
articles.expand: The OECD projected on Wednesday that the Bank of Japan will continue steadily normalizing monetary policy, with the short-term policy rate expected to rise from the current 0.75% to 2% by the end of 2027. In its latest Economic Survey of Japan, the OECD argued that resilient domestic demand, sustained wage growth, and improving inflation […] ...

articles.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: Inflation Is Moving the Wrong Way, Labor Market Is Not
articles.expand: Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee warned overnight that US inflation is moving in the “wrong way,” expressing particular concern that price pressures are spreading beyond energy and tariffs into broader service categories. His remarks followed April’s firmer-than-expected CPI report, which showed both headline (3.8%) and core (2.8%) inflation a...

articles.title: OECD sees BOJ hiking rates to 2% by end-2027 as Japan exits deflation era
articles.expand: The OECD projects the Bank of Japan will raise its policy rate to 2% by end-2027 from 0.75% currently, backing continued hikes on solid wage growth and a closed output gap. Summary: The OECD projects the Bank of Japan will raise its short-term policy rate to 2% by the end of 2027, up from the current 0.75%, supported by higher inflation expectation...

articles.title: Asia open: US inflation reaccelerates to 3.8%, and chip stocks falter
articles.expand: US inflation surged to 3.8% in April, the highest level in three years, wiping out Federal Reserve rate cut expectations and pushing Treasury yields and the US dollar higher. articles.title: Forex Today: Hot US CPI boosts US Dollar as Treasury yields climb
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) rallied toward the 98.30 region on Tuesday as hotter-than-expected United States (US) inflation data boosted Treasury yields and reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) may keep interest rates elevated for longer.

Wes Streeting is expected to meet with Starmer tomorrow morning and ask how he expects to solve the problems. articles.title: British Pound Slides as US Inflation Surprises and UK Political Uncertainty Mounts
articles.expand: BitcoinWorld British Pound Slides as US Inflation Surprises and UK Political Uncertainty Mounts The British Pound weakened against the US Dollar on Wednesday, extending its recent decline after a stronger-than-expected US inflation report reshaped expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate policy. Simultaneously, renewed political uncertainty i...

articles.title: Starmer and Ramaphosa are becoming the problem they promised to fix
articles.expand: Starmer and Ramaphosa rose to power as corrective figures, promising stability after years of chaos and institutional decay. Now both risk becoming symbols of drift themselves, as public frustration, weak growth and populist challengers expose the widening gap between political caution and effective leadership.

articles.title: The GBPUSD is the biggest mover today. Sellers taking back some control

The pair is down around -0.65% on the day, with sellers maintaining control after key technical breaks during the Asian-Pacific and European sessions. Tech...

articles.title: EURUSD breaks below 200 hour MA. Short term bias shift
articles.expand: The EURUSD is breaking below its 200-hour moving average at 1.17367 (green line on the chart below), a move that tilts the short-term bias more to the downside. articles.title: The US Dollar rallies back after CPI, is the correction is over ? – EUR/USD, GBP/USD & Dollar Index (DXY) overview

articles.title: NZDJPY Flashback Update: Preparing For Another Wave Higher
articles.expand: NZDJPY remains below the 95 resistance zone and could still be forming a wave four correction before another push higher toward 96–97.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.expand: The USDCHF continues to lean against its 100-hour moving average as a key resistance barometer. articles.title: Weekly Forex Forecast – AUD/USD, S&P 500 Index, NASDAQ 100 Index, Brent Crude Oil, Gasoline
articles.expand: As the prospects of a peace deal between the USA and Iran seemed to rise again, risk assets and stocks rose last week, while energy prices took a hit. Find out if a turnaround might happen this week as we face another Trump war deadline due Thursday.
